description: Content-format (AEAD) registry: the authenticated-encryption constructions selectable on the wire via an encryption envelope's `aead` field, encrypting sealed-PoE content under the content `payload_key`. Exactly one identifier is registered under `enc.scheme: 1`. The pinned `nonce_length_bytes` is the exact `enc.nonce` length the structural validator requires for that identifier; any other length is a NONCE_LENGTH_MISMATCH. An `aead` naming an identifier the implementation does not support follows the unknown-envelope rule: in the default public reading the envelope becomes opaque and the item is tagged ENC_UNSUPPORTED while its content-hash claim still validates; the recipient verifier and strict sealed-crypto mode reject with UNSUPPORTED_AEAD_ALG. Naming an unauthenticated cipher (CBC/CTR/ECB/CFB/OFB block modes in any key-size spelling, or legacy RC4/DES/3DES) is the more specific UNAUTHENTICATED_CIPHER_FORBIDDEN. Note: chacha20-poly1305 with a fixed zero nonce is NOT a wire-selectable content format and is deliberately not an entry here — it is the fixed per-slot key-wrap construction (wrapping the content-encryption key to a recipient slot, producing a 48-byte `wrap` = 32-byte wrapped key + 16-byte tag), not a selectable content cipher. aes-256-gcm is reserved for a future `enc.scheme` profile and is not registered under v1; a record naming it follows the unknown-envelope rule. Additions are additive-only and must ship with conformance vectors; a different content format is a new sealed-PoE construction version (`enc.scheme: 2`), never a reinterpretation of scheme 1.

entries.description: ChaCha20-Poly1305 (RFC 8439) in the 64 KiB segmented STREAM layout of age v1: every non-final chunk carries exactly 65536 plaintext bytes, and each chunk is sealed under the content `payload_key` with the 12-byte per-chunk nonce `uint88_be(counter) ‖ final_flag` (counter starts at 0, +1 per chunk; final_flag is 0x01 on the final chunk, 0x00 otherwise) and an empty per-chunk AAD, producing a 16-byte tag per chunk. The 24-byte `enc.nonce` is not a chunk nonce: it is the envelope-unique salt of the `payload_key` HKDF, which is what makes the counter nonces safe — `payload_key` is single-use, so no two streams ever share a (key, nonce) pair and stateless producers never coordinate nonces across envelopes. The segmented layout lets a verifier authenticate and release large payloads incrementally with bounded memory. The sole MANDATORY-to-implement content format for `enc.scheme: 1`; producers MUST emit this identifier, byte-exact.
entries.reference: ChaCha20-Poly1305 (RFC 8439) in the segmented STREAM layout of the age v1 specification (C2SP age.md)
